title: ledum glandulosum subsp. columbianum
intro: Ledum glandulosum subsp. columbianum, also known as Columbian Labrador Tea, is a subspecies of the Ledum glandulosum plant, a type of evergreen shrub native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Columbian Labrador Tea include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and a natural remedy for wounds. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
